case.description: Auth setup wizard fix + Prisma enum generator - database/config files below noise threshold
case.rationale: Call-graph analysis: setup-view.tsx, schema.prisma, package.json, and yarn.lock have no function call relationships connecting them. Multiple disconnected files with no call-graph connectivity = 3+ components = ERROR.
case.notes: Under domain-based classification, noise threshold filtered small changes leaving 1 domain. Under call-graph connectivity, these files are independent with no import/call relationships, forming multiple disconnected components.

case.description: IP resolution fix spanning auth API route, utility lib, and test file - test imports production API types
case.rationale: Call-graph analysis: getIP.ts exports getIP(), route.ts imports and calls getIP(), and getIP.test.ts imports the same function for testing. All files are connected via function call relationships = single connected component = PASS.
case.notes: Improvement over domain-based classification. Previously flagged as warning (auth+api domains), but call-graph analysis correctly identifies that all files are connected through shared function calls (getIP). Test file importing production types is a natural call-graph edge.

case.description: CI workflow only - GitHub Actions files only, single ci domain
case.rationale: All changed files are under .github/workflows/ which classifies as ci domain. Single domain = PASS.
case.notes: Dependabot bump for actions/upload-artifact. Classic single-domain CI change.

case.description: Docs-only PR - single .md file change
case.rationale: Only Readme.md changed, classified as docs domain. Single domain = PASS.
case.notes: README fix. docs domain = PASS.

case.description: package.json only - classified as unknown domain (ignored)
case.rationale: package.json does not match any domain pattern (not .toml/.yaml/.yml/.env, not in /config/). Classified as unknown domain which is ignored. 0 active domains = PASS.
case.notes: FALSE NEGATIVE example: package.json is a config file conceptually, but ghlint classifies it as unknown. A PR bumping a vulnerable dependency in package.json passes even if mixed with API changes, because package.json itself doesn't count.

case.description: Pure API response handler optimization - Express res.send() Content-Type deduplication
case.rationale: Two files changed: lib/response.js (core Express response handler) and History.md (changelog/docs). Path classification: lib/response.js = api domain (Express web framework core). History.md = docs domain but is changelog noise. Semantic analysis: response.js has no new imports (modifies existing code flow). With noise threshold applied, docs contribution from History.md (5 lines) is negligible vs api code (6+13 lines). Effective single domain = PASS.
case.notes: Tests that clean single-domain import detection works for CommonJS/no-import patterns. The response.js file uses require() style but is clearly API domain by path. History.md changelog should be filtered by noise threshold.

case.description: Test-only PR - test domain is always ignored by ghlint
case.rationale: All files are in test/ directory, classified as test domain which is always ignored. 0 active domains = PASS.
case.notes: Edge case: test domain is excluded from domain count. A PR touching ONLY tests always passes regardless of scope.

case.description: API-only PR - single routing.py file
case.rationale: fastapi/routing.py contains 'route' substring, classified as api domain. Single domain = PASS.
case.notes: Bug fix in router. Classic single api-domain change.

case.description: CSS/JS files under docs/ directory - path prefix beats extension
case.rationale: docs/en/docs/css/custom.css and docs/en/docs/js/custom.js both start with docs/ prefix, so they are classified as docs domain (not ui/unknown). Single domain = PASS.
case.notes: Edge case: .css file normally would be ui domain, but docs/ prefix is checked first. Path prefix takes priority over file extension.

case.description: Config-only change - single .go-version file with no code imports at all
case.rationale: Single file changed: .go-version (1 line: version string bump from 1.26.0 to 1.26.1). No imports, no code, no library references. Path classification: .go-version = config domain (dotfile version config). Semantic import analysis has nothing to analyze - no imports exist. Falls back entirely to path-based classification. 1 domain = PASS.
case.notes: Pure path-only fallback case. File contains only a version string - no imports, no code structure to analyze semantically. Verifies that the semantic analyzer gracefully handles files with zero parseable imports and does not interfere with path classification.

case.description: Go stdlib-only change - statefulset error wrapping with only fmt standard library usage
case.rationale: Single file changed: pkg/controller/statefulset/stateful_pod_control.go (4 additions). Changes only fmt.Errorf format verbs from %s to %w for proper error wrapping. All imports are Go standard library (fmt) and internal k8s packages. No third-party library imports that map to any domain. Path classification: pkg/controller/ could be api or unknown. Semantic analysis: only stdlib imports, no domain-specific library signals. Falls back to path classification. 1 domain = PASS.
case.notes: Go standard library imports (fmt, context, etc.) should not trigger any domain classification. The semantic analyzer should recognize these as domain-neutral and yield no hints, letting path-based classification decide. Tests that Go stdlib is correctly treated as non-signal.

case.description: Next.js evals standardization PR touching ui/database/docs/config/api domains
case.rationale: Eval test fixtures contain .tsx pages (ui), prisma schema (database), README.md (docs), package.json (config), api routes (api). Genuinely multi-domain PR that should be flagged.
case.notes: Large PR (5 domains). Evals are test-like but live under evals/ not test/, so not classified as test.

case.description: Next.js feature flag PR - UI components mixed with config changes
case.rationale: .tsx files in client/ and server/app-render/ = ui domain. server/config.ts = config domain (contains 'config' substring). 2 unrelated domains = WARNING.
case.notes: Feature flag implementation requires touching both config schema and render components. This is a common pattern in Next.js that gets flagged. May indicate the feature flag should be separated into its own PR.

case.description: Next.js vendor update for @vercel/og - compiled types (ui) + package.json (config)
case.rationale: Call-graph analysis: compiled .d.ts type definitions and package.json/pnpm-lock.yaml have no function call relationships. Type declaration files are generated artifacts with no import edges to package manifests. 3+ disconnected components = ERROR.
case.notes: Previously warning (ui+config = 2 domains). Call-graph analysis is stricter here: compiled type definitions and package config files have no call-graph edges connecting them, resulting in multiple disconnected components.

case.description: Prisma DB adapter refactor touching database adapters, docs, and config
case.rationale: conversion.ts and query-interpreter.ts import @prisma/ packages → semantic analysis correctly classifies as database (133 lines). README.md = docs (10 lines). package.json files = config (84 lines). 3 domains above noise threshold = ERROR.
case.notes: Tree-sitter semantic analysis correctly identifies @prisma/ imports → database domain. This PR is genuinely multi-domain: adapter code + docs + config.

case.description: Prisma init --db migration touching auth, database, and config
case.rationale: auth.ts = auth (8 lines). auth-client.ts = database (10 lines, contains database client logic). package.json + pnpm-lock.yaml = config (62 lines). 3 domains = ERROR.
case.notes: auth-client.ts classified as database despite 'auth' in name - worth investigating if this is correct or a false positive from semantic analysis.

case.description: Pure database adapter fix - MariaDB relationJoins capability check with only ORM imports
case.rationale: Call-graph analysis: mariadb.ts (production adapter code) and mariadb.test.ts (test file) form 2 disconnected components. The test file imports the adapter but call-graph analysis treats test files as separate components when they do not share transitive call edges with production code = 2 components = WARNING.
case.notes: Previously pass (single database domain). Call-graph analysis identifies the test file as disconnected from production code, forming 2 components. This is a stricter but defensible interpretation: test and production code are separate scoping concerns.

case.description: Prisma platform status command - UI components + database client code
case.rationale: Call-graph analysis: CLI output rendering files, management API client code, and supporting utilities form 3+ disconnected components with no shared call-graph edges. No single function call chain connects all changed files = ERROR.
case.notes: Previously warning (ui+database = 2 domains). Call-graph connectivity reveals 3+ independent clusters of files, escalating from warning to error.

case.description: Prisma port fixes for 7.4 - database+docs+ci+config (4 domains)
case.rationale: Database adapter code = database (247 lines). README.md = docs (56 lines). GitHub workflows = ci (95 lines). package.json files = config (85 lines). 4 domains = ERROR.
case.notes: Port/backport PRs are inherently multi-domain. Legitimate detection.

case.description: Prisma remove memory tests - database+docs+ci (3 domains)
case.rationale: Test files classified as database (111 lines, __tests__/ not in test patterns). README = docs (51 lines). Workflows = ci (91 lines). 3 domains = ERROR.
case.notes: FALSE POSITIVE: test files under __tests__/ should be test domain (ignored). Without this bug, would be docs+ci = WARNING. Known gap: __tests__ pattern.

case.description: Prisma Studio update - test file classified as database + config
case.rationale: Studio.vitest.ts under __tests__/ but not matching test path patterns = database (122 lines). package.json + pnpm-lock = config (13 lines). 2 domains = WARNING.
case.notes: __tests__/ directory not recognized as test path. This is a FALSE POSITIVE - the file is a test. Known gap: __tests__ pattern missing from diff_parser.zig.

case.description: shadcn-ui accordion aria-disabled fix - ui + config
case.rationale: Call-graph analysis: accordion component .tsx file and JSON config files have no function call relationships. TSX components do not import or call into JSON config files. 3+ disconnected components (tsx + multiple json config files) = ERROR.
case.notes: Previously warning (ui+config = 2 domains). Call-graph analysis finds no import/call edges between .tsx components and .json configuration files, resulting in 3+ disconnected components.

case.description: shadcn-ui major feature release - 5 domains across ui/docs/ci/config/api
case.rationale: Component files = ui (117k lines). README/docs = docs (105 lines). Workflows = ci (40 lines). package.json = config (173k lines). API routes = api (5k lines). 5 domains = ERROR.
case.notes: Very large release PR. Legitimate multi-domain detection.

case.description: shadcn-ui format run on all components - auth+ui+config from path patterns
case.rationale: Some components under auth-like paths = auth. .tsx components = ui. package.json files = config. 3 domains = ERROR.
case.notes: FALSE POSITIVE: formatting-only change should arguably be pass. But ghlint correctly detects the paths touch multiple domains. Scope issue, not classification issue.

case.description: shadcn-ui radio group style update - components (ui) + config
case.rationale: Call-graph analysis: radio group component .tsx files and package.json have no function call relationships. Components do not import or call into package manifests. Disconnected components = ERROR.
case.notes: Previously warning (ui+config = 2 domains). Call-graph analysis treats .tsx components and package.json as disconnected since there are no import/call edges between them.

case.description: shadcn-ui sidebar tooltip fix - .tsx components (ui) + .json style configs (config)
case.rationale: Call-graph analysis: sidebar.tsx component and .json style definition files have no function call relationships. TSX files do not import JSON style files via call-graph edges. Multiple disconnected components = ERROR.
case.notes: Previously warning (ui+config = 2 domains). The .json style/registry files are logically related to the UI component but have no call-graph connectivity, making them separate components under the new analysis.

case.description: UI-only PR - single React component fix
case.rationale: Single .tsx file in components/ path, classified as ui domain. Single domain = PASS.
case.notes: CSS class fix in a single component. Ideal focused PR.

case.description: Marketing event pages with session naming + schema file - multiple disconnected TSX/TS files
case.rationale: Call-graph analysis: multiple .tsx event pages and schemas.ts have no shared function call or import relationships. Each file forms an independent component = 3+ components = ERROR.
case.notes: Previously pass under domain-based classification (all files mapped to single UI domain). Call-graph approach reveals these files are structurally independent despite being in the same visual domain.

case.description: Self-hosted auth guides mixed with UI components and docker config
case.rationale: Call-graph analysis: self-hosting guide docs, NavigationMenu.constants.ts, and docker-compose.yml form multiple disconnected components. Documentation files, UI constants, and Docker config have no shared function call relationships = 3+ components = ERROR.
case.notes: Previously error under domain-based classification (auth+ui+config = 3 domains). Still error under call-graph analysis but for a different reason: multiple disconnected components with no import/call edges between them.

case.description: OAuth refactor with docs guide update - auth domain mixed with UI components
case.rationale: Call-graph analysis: docs auth guide files, OAuthApp*.tsx UI components, and supporting utilities form 3+ disconnected components. Documentation markdown files have no call-graph edges to TSX components = ERROR.
case.notes: Previously warning (auth+ui = 2 domains). Call-graph analysis identifies 3+ disconnected clusters: docs files, UI components, and any supporting files without shared import chains.

case.description: CI workflows + lockfile in same PR - ci and config domains mixed
case.rationale: Call-graph analysis: CI workflow YAML files and workspace config files are connected through shared configuration references. Workflow files reference workspace packages defined in pnpm-workspace.yaml, forming a connected component = PASS.
case.notes: Improvement over domain-based classification. Previously flagged as warning (ci+config = 2 domains), but call-graph analysis correctly identifies that CI workflows and workspace config are connected through shared references.

case.description: Supabase partner refactor - most files unknown, only login.tsx (auth) above threshold
case.rationale: Call-graph analysis: partner data files and login.tsx form one cluster, but api-types/types/platform.d.ts is disconnected with no call-graph edges to the other files. 2 disconnected components = WARNING.
case.notes: Previously pass (only 1 active domain with unknown files ignored). Call-graph analysis does not ignore unknown-classified files; it checks actual connectivity. The type definition file has no import relationship to the partner refactor files.

case.description: Docs site feature - UI components, API route, and docs bundled together
case.rationale: .tsx files in docs app = ui domain (232 lines). DEVELOPERS.md = docs domain (13 lines). app/api/.../route.ts contains 'api' and 'route' = api domain (22 lines). 3 domains = ERROR.
case.notes: Documentation site feature adding API endpoint + UI components + markdown docs. A genuinely mixed PR that should ideally be split into: (1) API endpoint, (2) UI components, (3) docs update.
